name: Chili Dog Rolls
recipeIngredient: 4 cups unbleached all-purpose flour (whole wheat flour)
1 large tomato, chopped OR 8 ounces tomato sauce
recipeInstructions:
Dissolve the yeast in 1/4 cup warm water and sugar or honey. Let stand a few minutes. Add 1 cup warm water and 1 tablespoon olive oil to the yeast mixture. Stir in the flour. Knead the dough on a floured surface for about 5 minutes, adding more flour if needed. Cover and let rise for one hour.
For the filling:
Mix the boiling water with TVP® granules or flakes. Let it stand while preparing the vegetables.
Heat a non-stick skillet and add 2 tablespoons olive oil. Saute the onions, pepper, and garlic until softened. Remove to a bowl.
Return the pan to heat and add another tablespoon of olive oil. Add the reconstituted TVP®, spices (cumin, chili powder, oregano), and salt. Cook for about a minute or two, then add the chopped tomato or tomato sauce.
Punch down the risen dough and divide into 2 balls. Place each ball on lightly oiled baking dishes.
On a floured surface, roll each dough ball out into an oblong shape about 5 inches wide. Spread half of the filling along the long side of the dough, leaving edges bare.
Roll the dough over to seal in the filling, pinching the edges. Cut each roll into 10 pieces and place the slices on baking sheets, seam side down. Let rise for another 20 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). Bake until lightly browned, about 20-25 minutes. Cool on a rack.
Per roll: 111 calories; 4 grams protein; 19 grams carbohydrates; 2 grams fat
